text:
On a small island off the coast of Florida lived a curious raccoon named Rusty. He loved adventure—a world full of mystery and endless possibilities. One cool autumn morning, as dawn broke through the dewy leaves, Rusty heard a rustling overhead.

Curiosity piqued, he scampered up to a tall, old oak where his ears perked up. There, sitting on a branch, was a peculiar creature. It had wings! But rather than flying, it was perched unnervingly on a fallen branch. Rusty crept closer, fascinated by this new specimen. The thing fluttered its wings, making a noise much like a bird but with a strange, humming sound.

Deciding to engage, Rusty slowly swaggered forward, pretending he wasn't terrified at the stranger perched above him. The creature seemed unafraid. Instead of flying away, it tilted slightly forward, seemed to talk to Rusty with a series of melodious buzzing sounds.
